preamble: The transformation of voiced bilabial stop [b] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz] is a notable case of labial palatalization, where the place of articulation shifts significantly forward and becomes affricated.

examples.note: Bantu languages frequently show labial consonants becoming dental or alveolar affricates under the influence of the palatal glide [j] or front vowels.
Specific Romance varieties exhibit labial-to-affricate shifts.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Schenker, A. M. (1995). The Dawn of Slavic.
